prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Download the AE101 content tarball to ~/Downloads. Use Bash:

risks_fired.evidence: Direct Bash-tool invocation suppresses most preamble variants; Claude may open with a one-liner ('Running the download now…') but the mechanical task structure and short execution path keep this below the 30% threshold.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Prime the student above the prompt if preamble is observed in cohort: 'Claude will confirm before running; skim past the opening line and look for the file-exists confirmation.'

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I'm starting a six-module training called Agentic Engineering 101. Walk these in order,

risks_fired.evidence: 'no preamble' is present but a five-step numbered task reliably triggers a confirmatory opening ('I'll work through these in order…') in ~40% of runs. Explicit suppression reduces but does not eliminate the pattern.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add a body-side primer above the prompt: 'Claude may narrate the plan before acting; skim past the opening summary and look for step-1 output.' Pairs with the existing 'no preamble' instruction for belt-and-callout coverage.

risks_fired.evidence: CARVE-OUT APPLIED (2026-05-03 new callout). Step 4 elicitation ('Ask me for three trivial-and-visible candidate bugs') would invite a criteria-dump at med confidence in isolation. Two body callouts satisfy the carve-out shape: (1) pre-prompt within ~1 paragraph: 'When Claude gets to the bug screen, push back if it dumps all four criteria at once. One bug at a time, then the next.' (2) post-prompt immediately after: 'If Step 4's screening feels like Claude is asking everything at once, ask it to use the AskUserQuestion tool, or to give you the candidate-bug screen one bug at a time. Your call.' Both callouts name the failure mode, give a recovery move, and give student agency. Downgraded from med to low per carve-out rule; not blocking.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Carve-out satisfied — no prompt edit required. Body callout is the legal alternative shape per check_prompts.md §15.

risks_fired.evidence: Step 3 installs skills to '~/.claude/skills/' with no explicit overwrite instruction. If a same-named skill already exists, Claude will pause to ask before overwriting. Most first-time students won't have a collision, keeping this low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'If a skill with that name already exists at ~/.claude/skills/, overwrite it.' to the step 3 install instruction to remove the ambiguity that triggers the pause.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Report success on each step and tell me what you did' invites a self-summary after skill install. Claude may claim installation confirmed when the SKILL.md path check was approximate. Non-load-bearing for prework's teaching goal.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Not blocking at low confidence. If cohort feedback shows install-path confusion, add an explicit verification step: 'Show me the full absolute path to each installed SKILL.md.'
